rulings.Case Title: FUND RECOVERY SERVICES INC DBA ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE VS. PATRICIA J HUGHES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Answer To Pos-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 17, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FUND RECOVERY SERVICES INC DBA ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE Notice Of Motion To Compel Answer To Pos-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: NIC INSURANCE COMPANY VS. LIBERTY SURPLUS INSURANCE CO., A NEW YORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Liberty Surplus Insurance Co'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 17, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF NAVIGATORS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Liberty Surplus Insurance Co's Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three; Request For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Pro Tem Judge Woodson as follows: Denied. For Request No. 13, the documents produced to date concerning Policy DGL-SF-184273-034, the policy in force at the time of the underlying accident, are responsive to the Request. For Request No. 15, the contents of the underwriting file for Policy DGL-SF-184273-034 are not in dispute in this coverage matter, and therefore are irrelevant. Consequently, production of the underwriting file is not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The parties sufficiently fulfilled their obligations to meet and confer. Monetary sanctions are denied. The Court has not considered Topa Insurance Company's joinder, filed by on October 2, 2012, as it is untimely. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN WILLIAMS VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 17, 2012, Line 16, PETITIONER BRIAN WILLIAMS' Motion For Writ Of Mandamus. Denied. Per the independent judgment standard yet giving a presumption of correctness to the ALJ's finidings, the weight of the evidence supports the determination that Petitioner did not suffer from an industrially-related physical condition that rendered him substantially unable to perform the usual duties of his position. Although the specifics of SFPD's offer of modified light duty in August/September 2009 are not entirely clear, Petitioner had been previously been assigned to modified light duty. Faced with returning to work, Petitioner made no further inquiry regarding the light duty assignment and categorically refused to return to work based on his own subjective belief of his unfitness, which was not substantiated by the medical evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 17, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES, GREG DEROSA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed to allege what FEHA protected activity they engaged in to support an actionable retaliation claim and what adverse employment action was taken against them to support an actionable claim of either retaliation or discrimination. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed to allege that any harassment they suffered was due to a characteristic of theirs protected by the FEHA. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed allege that any of their constitutional or statutory rights were interfered with by means of threats, intimidation or coercion. Because Plaintiffs were previously granted leave to amend to address the specified defects and their opposition papers do not suggest how they can cure these deficiencies, no basis appears to grant further leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
